gazette: Official Gazette dated 12 May 2023 and numbered 32188
title: Decision on the Approval of the Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) between the Republic of Turkey and the Republic of Uzbekistan (Decision Number: 7286)
bodyHtml:
The Decision (Number: 7286) on the Approval of the Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA)between the Republic of Turkey and the Republic of Uzbekistan has been published.

Under the agreement, Uzbekistan grants concessions to Turkey on 12 industrial products. These products include flat-rolled products of iron or steel, centrifugal pumps, pipes, boilers, tanks, etc., devices generating electricity from diesel, transformers, electric panels, certain machinery components and parts, electric conductors, control adjusting devices, and prefabricated buildings. Uzbekistan will provide a 60 percent  customs duty reduction for prefabricated buildings and a 50  percent reduction for the rest of the mentioned products.

Turkey, on the other hand, will grant customs duty reductions on 12 agricultural products subject to quantity limitations and limited production in Turkey. These products include onions, chickpeas, certain vegetable preserves (pickles, capers, sweet corn), dried peas, black beans (similar to mango), white beans, almonds, walnuts, dried black grapes, dried plums, dried peaches and pears, peppers, and peanuts.

Within this framework, while Uzbekistan reduces customs duties on certain industrial products, Turkey provides market access advantages through quota limitations on a limited number of agricultural products.

To benefit from the preferential regime, the value of foreign inputs used in the production of these products should not exceed:

20 percent of the EXW price for goods classified under codes 841370, 850213, 940690,

40 percent for other products.

The agreement also includes bilateral cumulation of origin, and the EUR.1 Movement Certificate will be used in the trade covered by the agreement.
